赎.origin: 赎 has the shell 贝 for money and means to redeem or ransom.

sentence.en: They want a ransom.

赏.origin: 赏 has the shell 贝 for money and means to reward or to admire.

sentence.en: The teacher rewarded him a star.

赐.origin: 赐 has the shell 贝 for money and means to bestow or grant.

sentence.en: The king granted him a horse.

赔.origin: 赔 has the shell 贝 for money and means to compensate or to lose money.

sentence.en: He lost quite a lot of money.

赖.origin: 赖 means to rely on, or to deny and not admit something.

sentence.en: Children depend on their parents.

赚.origin: 赚 has the shell 贝 for money and means to earn or make a profit.

sentence.en: He wants to earn more money.

赠.origin: 赠 has the shell 贝 for money and means to give a gift.

sentence.en: He gave me a book as a gift.

赣.origin: 赣 is the short name for Jiangxi province and a river there.

sentence.en: The Gan River is very long.

赤.origin: 赤 pictures a person glowing by a fire 火; it means red or bare.

sentence.en: He walks with bare feet.

赫.origin: 赫 is two red 赤 together, meaning bright and dazzling.

sentence.en: His family is very illustrious.

赴.origin: 赴 has the walk radical 走 and means to go to or to attend.

sentence.en: He went to keep the appointment.

赵.origin: 赵 has the walk radical 走 and is a very common family name.

sentence.en: Teacher Zhao has arrived.

起.origin: 起 has the walk radical 走 and means to rise or to get up.

sentence.en: I get up at six in the morning.

趁.origin: 趁 has the walk radical 走 and means to take advantage of a chance.

sentence.en: Let's go now while we can.

趋.origin: 趋 has the walk radical 走 and means to hurry toward.

sentence.en: The weather tends to be getting colder.

趟.origin: 趟 has the walk radical 走 and is used to count trips or times.

sentence.en: I made a trip to the store.

趣.origin: 趣 has the walk radical 走 and means interest or fun.

sentence.en: This story is very interesting.

趴.origin: 趴 has the foot radical 足 and means to lie face down.

word.en: to lie down on one's front

sentence.en: He lies on the desk to sleep.

趸.origin: 趸 has the foot radical 足 and means to buy or sell wholesale.

sentence.en: He bought a batch of goods wholesale.

跃.origin: 跃 has the foot radical 足 and means to leap or jump.

sentence.en: The little deer leaps on the grass.

跋.origin: 跋 has the foot radical 足 and means to trek over difficult ground.

sentence.en: They trekked a long way.

跌.origin: 跌 means the foot 足 loses its hold 失, so to fall down.

sentence.en: He fell down.

跛.origin: 跛 has the foot radical 足 and means lame, walking unevenly.

sentence.en: That dog is limping.

距.origin: 距 has the foot radical 足 and means distance or gap.

sentence.en: The two places are far apart.

跤.origin: 跤 has the foot radical 足 and means a tumble or fall.

sentence.en: He took a tumble.

跨.origin: 跨 has the foot radical 足 and means to step over or straddle.

sentence.en: He stepped over the ditch.

跷.origin: 跷 has the foot radical 足 and means to stand on tiptoe or to raise.

sentence.en: He raised his foot.

跺.origin: 跺 has the foot radical 足 and means to stamp the feet.

sentence.en: He stamped his feet in anger.

踏.origin: 踏 has the foot radical 足 and means to step on or tread.

sentence.en: Please do not step on the lawn.

踝.origin: 踝 has the foot radical 足 and means the ankle.

sentence.en: My ankle hurts.

踩.origin: 踩 has the foot radical 足 and means to step on.

sentence.en: I stepped on a stone.

踮.origin: 踮 has the foot radical 足 and means to stand on tiptoe.

sentence.en: She tiptoes to see the blackboard.

踱.origin: 踱 has the foot radical 足 and means to pace slowly.

sentence.en: He paces in the yard.

踹.origin: 踹 has the foot radical 足 and means to kick or trample.

sentence.en: He kicked the door open.

蹄.origin: 蹄 has the foot radical 足 and means a hoof.

sentence.en: The sound of the horse's hooves is loud.

蹈.origin: 蹈 has the foot radical 足 and means to stamp or to dance.

sentence.en: She is learning dance.

蹑.origin: 蹑 has the foot radical 足 and means to walk softly on tiptoe.

sentence.en: He walks softly on tiptoe.

蹦.origin: 蹦 has the foot radical 足 and means to bounce or hop.

sentence.en: The child bounces happily.

蹬.origin: 蹬 has the foot radical 足 and means to step or pedal hard.

sentence.en: He pedals the bike hard.

蹭.origin: 蹭 has the foot radical 足 and means to shuffle or rub along.

sentence.en: The kitten rubs against my leg.

蹲.origin: 蹲 has the foot radical 足 and means to squat or crouch.

sentence.en: He squats down to tie his shoes.

蹼.origin: 蹼 has the foot radical 足 and means webbed feet, like a duck's.

sentence.en: Ducks have webbed feet.

蹿.origin: 蹿 has the foot radical 足 and means to spring up or dart.

sentence.en: The cat sprang up the wall in one go.

躬.origin: 躬 means to bend the body 身 like a bow 弓, so to bow.

sentence.en: He bows to the teacher.

躲.origin: 躲 has the body radical 身 and means to hide or to dodge.

sentence.en: He is hiding behind the door.

轧.origin: 轧 has the cart radical 车 and means to roll over or crush.

sentence.en: The car rolled over the stone.

轨.origin: 轨 has the cart radical 车 and means a track or rail.

sentence.en: The train runs on the tracks.

转.origin: 转 has the cart radical 车; read zhuǎn it means to turn or change.

sentence.en: He turned around and left.

轮.origin: 轮 has the cart radical 车 and means a wheel.

sentence.en: The wheel is broken.

软.origin: 软 has the cart radical 车 and means soft and flexible.

sentence.en: This pillow is very soft.
